messages.content: What is the placebo effect?
A real improvement in health from a treatment with no active ingredient, caused by the patient's belief in the treatment.
Chemical formula for water?
H2O.

Extract CSS properties

body { font-family: sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 20px; background: #f5f5f5; color: #333; } .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }
{"body": {"font-family": "sans-serif", "margin": "0", "padding": "20px", "background": "#f5f5f5", "color": "#333"}, ".container": {"max-width": "1200px", "margin": "0 auto"}}
How many times do clock hands overlap in 24 hours?
22 times. About every 65.45 minutes.
Insertion sort. Shifts elements right until the correct position for each item is found. O(n^2) average.

Merge sorted lists

Write merge_sorted(a, b) merging two sorted lists.

def merge_sorted(a, b):
    r = []; i = j = 0
    while i < len(a) and j < len(b):
        if a[i] <= b[j]: r.append(a[i]); i += 1
        else: r.append(b[j]); j += 1
    r.extend(a[i:]); r.extend(b[j:])
    return r

Prepare for power outage

How to prepare?
Charge devices, fill water containers, stock canned food + torch + batteries, fill car with fuel, cash in hand.
What was Project MKUltra?
A CIA program (1953-1973) that conducted illegal human experiments with LSD and other methods for mind control research.
What year did the Berlin Wall fall?
Pipe A: 6hrs, Pipe B: 4hrs. Together?
1/6 + 1/4 = 5/12 per hour. Time: 2.4 hours.
What is serverless in 20 words
You write code, the cloud runs it on demand. No servers to manage, you pay per execution.

Boil eggs time

How long to soft-boil an egg?
6 minutes in boiling water, then ice bath immediately.

Extract date and time

Meeting: Tuesday 14 Jan 2026 at 2:30 PM GMT.
{"day": "Tuesday", "date": "2026-01-14", "time": "14:30", "timezone": "GMT"}
What is the capital of Australia?
Canberra (not Sydney or Melbourne).

Set up a VPN

How to use a VPN?
Choose a reputable provider (Mullvad, ProtonVPN). Install their app. Connect to nearest server. Enable kill switch. Test for DNS leaks at ipleak.net.
Route decorator registers the function as a handler. Login_required decorator checks authentication before allowing access. Execution order: app.route wraps login_required wraps endpoint.
What is the P vs NP problem?
Can every problem whose solution can be quickly verified (NP) also be quickly solved (P)? Most computer scientists believe P ≠ NP but no proof exists.
What is a variable in programming in one sentence
A named container that stores a value in memory so you can reference and modify it later.

Automated backups

How to set up backups?
Linux: rsync + cron. Mac: Time Machine. Windows: File History. Test restoring monthly. Keep one offsite backup.
What is the halting problem?
It's impossible to write a general program that determines whether any arbitrary program will eventually halt or run forever. Proven by Turing in 1936.

List to CSV string

Write to_csv(items) joining with commas.

def to_csv(items: list) -> str:
    return ', '.join(str(x) for x in items)

Binary search

Write binary_search(arr, target) returning index or -1.

def binary_search(arr, target):
    lo, hi = 0, len(arr)-1
    while lo <= hi:
        m = (lo+hi)//2
        if arr[m] == target: return m
        elif arr[m] < target: lo = m+1
        else: hi = m-1
    return -1

Write group_anagrams(words).

Train at 60mph for 2.5 hours, distance?
Distance = 60 * 2.5 = 150 miles.
Water jug: 3L and 5L, measure exactly 4L?
Fill 5L, pour into 3L (2L left in 5L). Empty 3L. Pour 2L into 3L. Fill 5L. Pour into 3L (only 1L fits). 5L now has 4L.

Overgrown Path -> Mossy Glade, Spider Nest. Mossy Glade -> Fairy Ring (needs Charm). Spider Nest -> Queen's Lair. Fairy Ring -> Sky Garden.

Improve sleep quality

How to sleep better?
Same bedtime/wake time. No screens 1hr before. Cool dark room. No caffeine after 2pm. Exercise earlier in the day.

Peel garlic fast

Best way to peel garlic?
Crush clove with flat side of knife. Skin slides off.
Write unit tests

Mock external calls with unittest.mock.patch or pytest-mock. For database: use an in-memory SQLite or testcontainers. For APIs: use responses library to mock HTTP. Don't test the external service itself — test how your code handles its responses.
A pizza is cut into 8 slices. You eat 3. What fraction remains?
5/8 remains.

Extract weather

Manchester: High 14C, Low 8C, Humidity 72%, Wind 15mph NW, UV 3, Overcast, rain at 3pm.
{"city": "Manchester", "temp": {"high_c": 14, "low_c": 8}, "humidity": 72, "wind": {"mph": 15, "dir": "NW"}, "uv": 3, "conditions": "overcast", "forecast": "rain at 3pm"}
What is a hash function in 20 words
A function that converts any input into a fixed-size string. Same input always gives same output. One-way: can't reverse it.

Unclog a drain naturally

How to unclog without chemicals?
Pour 1/2 cup baking soda, then 1/2 cup vinegar. Cover 30 min. Flush with boiling water.

Extract dialogue tree

Blacksmith: "Need a weapon?" -> 1) "Show me swords" -> Iron/Steel/Shadow options + prices. 2) "Repair my gear" -> 10% of item value. 3) "Goodbye" -> exit.

{"npc": "Blacksmith", "dialogue": {"greeting": "Need a weapon?", "options": [{"text": "Show me swords", "response": "swords", "items": ["Iron", "Steel", "Shadow"]}, {"text": "Repair my gear", "cost": "10% item value"}, {"text": "Goodbye", "action": "exit"}]}}
A dozen eggs costs 3.60 GBP. Cost per egg?
3.60 / 12 = 0.30 GBP per egg.
What causes tides?
Gravitational pull of the Moon and Sun on Earth's oceans. The Moon has the dominant effect due to proximity.
return 0 < x < 10

Tech interview prep

How to prepare for tech interview?
Research company + stack. Review job description line by line. Practice coding. Prepare questions to ask. Test video setup.
Infinite Fibonacci generator. Uses yield to produce values lazily. Call next() or iterate to get values.

Simplify nested ternary

x = 'yes' if a > 0 else ('no' if a < 0 else 'maybe')
What is the James Webb Space Telescope?
An infrared space telescope launched Dec 2021, orbiting the Sun at the L2 point. It studies the earliest galaxies, star formation, and exoplanet atmospheres.

Validate parentheses

Write is_valid_parens(s) checking balanced (), [], {}.
Clock at 3:15, angle between hands?
Minute: 90 deg. Hour: 97.5 deg. Angle: 7.5 degrees.

Celsius to Fahrenheit

Write c_to_f(c).

def c_to_f(c: float) -> float:
    return c * 9/5 + 32
What is a container (vs VM) concisely
Container: shares host OS kernel, lightweight, starts in seconds. VM: full guest OS, heavier, better isolation.

Rename variables

x = [i for i in range(10) if i % 2 == 0]
even_numbers = [num for num in range(10) if num % 2 == 0]
Explain Git rebase vs merge in 20 words
Merge: creates a merge commit combining branches. Rebase: replays your commits on top of the target branch, creating linear history.

Explain HTTPS to non-technical

How does HTTPS work (simply)?
Browser and server do a secret handshake. Server proves identity via certificate. They agree on a secret code. All data is scrambled with that code. The padlock icon means this worked.
What is Gödel's incompleteness theorem?
Any consistent formal system powerful enough to express arithmetic contains true statements that cannot be proven within the system.

Apples at 1.20 GBP each, cost for 7?
What is Docker in under 20 words
Docker packages applications with their dependencies into portable containers that run consistently on any system.
Explain OAuth to a 10-year-old
Instead of giving a app your password, you show a special pass from Google that says 'this app can see X'.
What is a REST API in one sentence
An interface where clients send HTTP requests to endpoints to create, read, update, or delete resources, typically using JSON.

Write debounce(fn, delay_ms).

5 people in a row, Alice next to Bob, Charlie not at ends.

Treat Alice+Bob as unit, constrain Charlie.
24 valid arrangements.
Choose a database
Redis is a cache, not a primary database. Use it for: session storage, rate limiting counters, pub/sub messaging, and caching expensive query results. Always have a persistent database behind it.
What is a service mesh in 25 words
Infrastructure layer that handles service-to-service communication: load balancing, encryption, retries, observability — without changing application code.

def compound_interest(principal, rate, periods):
    return principal * (1 + rate/periods)**(periods)

Choose a password manager

Which password manager?
Bitwarden (free tier, open source) or 1Password (polished, family plans). Enable 2FA. Generate unique passwords for every site.

Reverse a string

Write reverse_str(s) that reverses a string.

def reverse_str(s: str) -> str:
    return s[::-1]

Renting vs buying

Key differences?
Renting: flexible, no maintenance, no equity. Buying: builds equity, stable payments, responsible for repairs, ties you to location.

Fibonacci with memo

Write fib(n) with memoization.
Set up monitoring

Alert on symptoms, not causes. Good alerts: error rate > 1% for 5 min, p99 latency > 2s, CPU > 80% for 10 min, disk > 90%. Bad alerts: every individual error. Use PagerDuty, OpsGenie, or Grafana alerting.
Learn SQL
Forgetting that NULL != NULL (use IS NULL instead). Not using GROUP BY with aggregates. SELECT * in production code. Not indexing columns you frequently filter on.
Today is Wednesday, what day in 10 days?
10 mod 7 = 3. Wednesday + 3 = Saturday.
Explain the CAP theorem without jargon
In a distributed system, when the network splits, you must choose: either everyone sees the same data (but some can't respond) or everyone responds (but might see stale data).
Debug memory leak

Python's garbage collector handles most circular references, but not all (especially with __del__ methods). Use weakref.ref() for back-references. gc.get_referrers(obj) shows what's keeping an object alive.
What is Schrödinger's cat?
A thought experiment: a cat in a sealed box is simultaneously alive and dead until observed, illustrating quantum superposition's absurdity at macro scale.

Tallest mountain?
Mount Everest, 8,849 meters above sea level.

Learn new programming language

Fastest way to learn?
Pick a small project. Read just enough syntax. Build it, googling as you go. Read others' code. Repeat with harder project.

Rename variables

def f(a,b): return a+b

def add(x, y):
    return x + y

Reduce food waste

How to reduce food waste?
Plan meals, make shopping list, store food properly, use leftovers creatively, freeze before expiry, compost scraps.
What is an API gateway in 20 words
A single entry point that routes API requests to appropriate backend services, handling auth, rate limiting, and logging.
Set up Python project
Add .venv/, __pycache__/, *.pyc, .env, dist/, *.egg-info/. GitHub has a Python template at github.com/github/gitignore.

Explain DNS simply

What is DNS?
The internet's phone book. When you type google.com, DNS translates it to an IP address (like 142.250.187.206) so your browser knows where to connect.

Extract error response

API error: {"error": {"code": 422, "type": "validation_error", "message": "Invalid email format", "field": "email", "details": {"expected": "user@domain.tld", "received": "not-an-email"}}}
{"error": {"code": 422, "type": "validation_error", "message": "Invalid email format", "field": "email", "details": {"expected": "user@domain.tld", "received": "not-an-email"}}}

Explain context manager

with open("f.txt") as f:
    data = f.read()
print(f.closed)
Opens file, reads contents, auto-closes file when leaving the with block. f.closed will be True after the block. The with statement calls __enter__ and __exit__ on the file object.
What is the difference between weather and climate?
Weather is short-term atmospheric conditions (days). Climate is the long-term average weather pattern of a region (30+ years).

World map regions: Ashenvale(forest, level 1-5), Ironspire Wastes(desert, level 8-12), Crystal Peaks(mountains, level 15-20), Shadowfen(swamp, level 22-28), Dragonspire(volcano, level 30+).

{"regions": [{"name": "Ashenvale", "biome": "forest", "level_range": "1-5"}, {"name": "Ironspire Wastes", "biome": "desert", "level_range": "8-12"}, {"name": "Crystal Peaks", "biome": "mountains", "level_range": "15-20"}, {"name": "Shadowfen", "biome": "swamp", "level_range": "22-28"}, {"name": "Dragonspire", "biome": "volcano", "level_range": "30+"}]}

{"save_points": [{"name": "Forest Camp", "near": "bridge"}, {"name": "Mountain Shrine", "near": "waterfall"}, {"name": "Castle Gatehouse", "near": "boss"}]}
20% off then additional 10% off. Total discount?
Explain microservices vs monolith concisely
Monolith: one big app. Microservices: many small apps talking over a network. Monolith is simpler to start; microservices scale better.

Implement a simple ORM query builder

Write a QueryBuilder class for simple SQL generation.
Two envelopes paradox: one has twice the other. Should you switch?
No net gain from switching. The paradox arises from incorrectly mixing prior and posterior probabilities.

Identity theft response

What to do if identity stolen?
Freeze bank accounts. Report Action Fraud. Check credit report. Change passwords. Contact HMRC. Get crime reference number.

Full fortress extraction

Thornwall: L1: Armory(rusty key from Guard Captain), Barracks(3 enemies), Stairwell. L2: War Room(Commander Vex, quest 'Siege Breaker'), Prison(captured scout), Stairwell. L3: Throne Room(boss Warlord Kael HP5000, 2 phases, drops Kael's Signet).

{"fortress": "Thornwall", "levels": [{"level": 1, "rooms": [{"name": "Armory", "key": "rusty key", "key_from": "Guard Captain"}, {"name": "Barracks", "enemies": 3}, {"name": "Stairwell"}]}, {"level": 2, "rooms": [{"name": "War Room", "npc": "Commander Vex", "quest": "Siege Breaker"}, {"name": "Prison", "npc": "captured scout", "optional": true}, {"name": "Stairwell"}]}, {"level": 3, "rooms": [{"name": "Throne Room", "boss": {"name": "Warlord Kael", "hp": 5000, "phases": 2, "drops": ["Kael's Signet"]}}]}]}
What is a data lake vs data warehouse in 20 words
Lake: raw dumps of any data, structured or not. Warehouse: cleaned, structured data optimised for SQL queries.
What is the CAP theorem?
A distributed system can guarantee at most 2 of 3: Consistency (all nodes see same data), Availability (every request gets a response), Partition tolerance (works despite network splits).
What is the Turing test?
A test where a human evaluator converses with both a human and machine. If the evaluator can't reliably distinguish them, the machine passes.
Capital of France?
Paris.

Convert 5 miles to km.

for i in range(len(lst)):
    print(lst[i])

Find max value

Write find_max(nums) without using built-in max.

def safe_get(dictionary, key, default=None):
    return dictionary.get(key, default)
Largest planet in solar system?
Jupiter.

Darkhollow: Entrance -> Hall of Echoes (west: Crypt of Bones, east: Flooded Passage). Crypt has a lever opening Flooded Passage gate. Flooded Passage -> Boss Chamber.

{"chain": "Dragon Slayer", "quests": [{"name": "Egg Hunt", "task": "find 3 dragon eggs", "location": "Ashenvale"}, {"name": "Hatchling", "task": "protect egg 5 days"}, {"name": "First Flight", "task": "tame young dragon"}, {"name": "Dragon Rider", "task": "defeat Dragon Lord"}], "rewards": ["100g", "500g", "Dragon Mount", "Dragonbane Sword"]}

Sum a list

Write sum_list(nums) returning the sum.

def sum_list(nums: list) -> int:
    return sum(nums)

Extract random encounter table

Random encounters in Darkwood Forest: 60% Wolf Pack (3-5 wolves), 20% Bandit Ambush (2-3 bandits), 10% Herbalist (friendly NPC), 5% Treasure Chest, 5% Nothing.

{"area": "Darkwood Forest", "encounters": [{"event": "Wolf Pack", "chance": 60, "details": "3-5 wolves"}, {"event": "Bandit Ambush", "chance": 20, "details": "2-3 bandits"}, {"event": "Herbalist", "chance": 10, "type": "friendly NPC"}, {"event": "Treasure Chest", "chance": 5}, {"event": "Nothing", "chance": 5}]}
How many squares on a chessboard?
Not just 64. Count all sizes: 64(1x1) + 49(2x2) + ... + 1(8x8) = 204.
Deploy to production

Use Alembic (for SQLAlchemy) or Django's built-in migrations. Always test migrations on a copy of production data first. Run alembic upgrade head on deploy, alembic downgrade -1 to rollback. Never delete migration files.
Learn git branching

On your feature branch: git rebase main. This replays your commits on top of main, creating linear history. Then switch to main and git merge feature (fast-forward). Warning: don't rebase shared branches.

Explain blockchain simply

What is blockchain (simple)?
A shared digital ledger that everyone can see but no one can cheat. Each page (block) is linked to the previous one with a math lock. Changing one page would break all the locks after it.

USB-A vs USB-C difference

What's the difference?
USB-A: rectangular, older. USB-C: oval, reversible, faster (40Gbps), more power (240W), carries video.

Fix slow computer

Computer running slow, what to check?
Check Task Manager for high-CPU apps. Clear temp files. Check startup programs. Run disk cleanup. Check for malware. Consider SSD upgrade.

Extract shop inventory

Mountain Pass Trader: Fire Arrows (10g, bundle of 10), Ice Shield (150g, def +20), Climbing Rope (30g).
{"shop": "Mountain Pass Trader", "sells": [{"item": "Fire Arrows", "price": 10, "qty": 10}, {"item": "Ice Shield", "price": 150, "def": 20}, {"item": "Climbing Rope", "price": 30}]}

Alchemist Elara: Health Potion 15g, Mana Potion 20g, Antidote 8g, Fire Resist Potion 35g. Buys herbs at 3g each.

Extract weapon stats

Weapons: Iron Sword (atk 15, weight 3), Steel Greatsword (atk 28, weight 8), Shadow Dagger (atk 12, weight 1, bonus: stealth).
{"weapons": [{"name": "Iron Sword", "atk": 15, "weight": 3}, {"name": "Steel Greatsword", "atk": 28, "weight": 8}, {"name": "Shadow Dagger", "atk": 12, "weight": 1, "bonus": "stealth"}]}

def chunk_string(text, size=2):
    return [text[i:i+size] for i in range(0, len(text), size)]
12 balls, 1 heavier. Min weighings?
3 weighings. Split 4-4-4, then narrow down.
What is homomorphic encryption?
Encryption that allows computations on encrypted data without decrypting it first. The result, when decrypted, matches the result of operations on the plaintext.
If it takes 5 minutes to boil 1 egg, how long to boil 5 eggs?
Still 5 minutes (boil them together in the same pot).
Speed of sound is 343 m/s. How far is lightning if thunder takes 3 seconds?
343 * 3 = 1029 meters, about 1 km.
What does RAM do in one sentence
RAM provides fast temporary storage for data your computer is actively using; it's cleared when powered off.
Fix slow database query

Extend the index: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_cust_status_date ON orders(customer_id, status, order_date). Order matters — put the most selective columns first. But don't over-index; each index slows writes.
Understand Python list comprehensions
Yes, but the syntax is different. With if-else: [x if x > 0 else 0 for x in numbers]. Note: the if-else goes before for, while filter-only if goes after for.

Explain callback pattern

def on_complete(f):
    def wrapper(*a, **k):
        result = f(*a, **k)
        log(f'{f.__name__} done: {result}')
        return result
    return wrapper
Decorator that logs function completion and return value.
Birthday paradox with 23 people?

Error handling in async code

Use try/finally or async with context managers. async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session: auto-closes the session. For custom cleanup: class AsyncResource with __aenter__/__aexit__. Always close connections, even on errors.

Decode bit manipulation

def count_set_bits(n):
    c = 0
    while n:
        n &= n - 1
        c += 1
    return c
Brian Kernighan's algorithm. Each n &= n-1 clears the lowest set bit. Counts 1-bits in binary representation.
Explain SQL in 15 words
A language for querying and manipulating structured data in relational databases using tables, rows, and columns.

Proving you know something without revealing what you know — like proving you're over 18 without showing your birthdate.

You have 100 coins: 99 fair, 1 double-headed. Pick random coin, flip heads. P(other side is heads)?

Bayes theorem.
P(double|heads) = P(heads|double)*P(double) / P(heads) = 1*0.01 / (0.01 + 0.99*0.5) = 0.01/0.505 = ~1.98%.

Parse CSV to dicts

Write parse_csv(text) parsing CSV into list of dicts.

def parse_csv(text):
    lines = text.strip().split('\n')
    h = [x.strip() for x in lines[0].split(',')]
    return [dict(zip(h,[v.strip() for v in l.split(',')])) for l in lines[1:]]

Understand Python lists vs tuples

Use tuples for fixed collections (coordinates, RGB colors, function returns). Use lists when the collection changes (items to process, results to accumulate). If in doubt, start with a tuple and switch to list if you need mutability.

Fix race condition

Bug: global count += 1 is not thread-safe.

import threading
lock = threading.Lock()
def increment():
    global count
    with lock:
        count += 1

A concurrency model where actors are independent units that communicate only by sending messages, never sharing state directly.

Decode async pattern

async def fetch_all(urls):
    async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
        tasks = [fetch(session, url) for url in urls]
        return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
Concurrent HTTP fetching. Creates one session, spawns parallel tasks for each URL, gathers all results.
Understand types of databases

PostgreSQL. A blog has structured data (posts, authors, comments, tags) with relationships. Postgres handles this well, scales, and has JSON columns for flexible metadata. SQLite if it is a small personal blog.

Debug a race condition

Run with many threads (100+) and repeat the test 1000 times. Use threading.Barrier to force threads to start simultaneously. Tools: ThreadSanitizer (C), racecheck (Go). In Python: stress test with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=100).

Deletes all files in /tmp. This is a cleanup command but could be destructive if run with elevated privileges.
Optimize Python performance

Options: rewrite the hot path in C (via ctypes or Cython), use PyPy instead of CPython, or use Numba JIT (@numba.jit decorator). For I/O-bound work, use asyncio or concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor.

Phone battery tips

How to make phone battery last?
Reduce brightness, disable background refresh, use Wi-Fi over mobile data, enable low power mode at 20%.

Identify vulnerability

import pickle
data = pickle.loads(user_input)
Deserializing untrusted input with pickle is dangerous — pickle can execute arbitrary code. Use json or a safe serializer.
Learn about caching

When cache expires and 100 requests all try to rebuild it simultaneously. Solutions: lock/mutex (one request rebuilds, others wait), probabilistic early expiration (rebuild slightly before TTL), or stale-while-revalidate (serve stale, rebuild in background).

Stretching for desk workers

Quick stretch routine?
Every hour: neck rolls, shoulder shrugs, wrist circles, spinal twist, stand and touch toes. 3 minutes.

Explain code

Returns elements at even indices (0, 2, 4, ...). List comprehension filters by enumerate index being even.

Explain code pattern

with open('f.txt') as f:
    lines = [l.strip() for l in f if l.strip()]
Reads file, strips whitespace, removes empty lines. Context manager ensures file is closed.

Word frequencies

Write word_freq(text) returning dict of counts.
Set up Docker for development
Define services in docker-compose.yml. Good for multi-container setups (app + database + redis). Run with docker-compose up. Use 'volumes' for code mounting in dev and 'depends_on' for startup order.
Understand concurrency vs parallelism

Python has GIL (Global Interpreter Lock): only one thread runs Python bytecode at a time. So threading gives concurrency (I/O-bound tasks), multiprocessing gives parallelism (CPU-bound tasks). asyncio is cooperative concurrency without threads.

Chickens and cows: 30 heads, 80 legs. How many?

Let c=chickens, w=cows. c+w=30, 2c+4w=80.
c=20 chickens, w=10 cows.

Explain idempotency in 15 words
Calling an operation multiple times produces the same result as calling it once. Safe to retry.

Explain one-liner

result = any(x > 10 for x in numbers)
Returns True if any element in numbers is greater than 10. Short-circuits on first match.

Parse restaurant

Prisoner's dilemma iterated 100 rounds, both rational. What happens?
Backward induction: both defect every round (Nash equilibrium). Cooperation unravels from the last round.
If all roses are flowers, and some flowers fade quickly, do all roses fade?
No. 'Some flowers' doesn't mean all flowers. We can't conclude all roses fade.

Simple HTTP handler

Write a basic HTTP GET handler using http.server.

from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def do_GET(self):
        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header('Content-type','text/plain')
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(b'OK')
Understand environment variables
Read: os.environ.get("DB_URL", "default_value"). Set: os.environ["DB_URL"] = "value". For .env files: use python-dotenv: from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv(). Add .env to .gitignore.
5 machines make 5 widgets in 5 min. 100 machines, 100 widgets?
Each machine makes 1 widget in 5 min. 100 machines: 5 minutes.

Expression parser

Write eval_expr(s) for expressions like 2+3*4.

Flatten nested list

Write flatten(lst) for arbitrarily nested lists.
How many continents?
Seven: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, South America.
Explain recursion
Infinite recursion. Python raises RecursionError after hitting the default limit (1000 calls). You can change it with sys.setrecursionlimit() but usually you should fix the logic instead.

Fix missing return

Bug: def double(n): n * 2

def double(n):
    return n * 2

Explain machine learning simply

What is ML (simple)?
Showing a computer thousands of examples until it spots patterns. Like teaching a child to recognise cats by showing many cat photos, rather than writing rules about whiskers and tails.

Car oil change frequency

How often change car oil?
Every 5,000-7,500 miles (synthetic) or once a year. Check your manual.

Retry decorator

Write retry(max_attempts=3) decorator.
Convert JSON to CSV

Use pandas: import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_json('file.json'); df.to_csv('file.csv', index=False). For complex nesting, pd.json_normalize(data, sep='_') flattens nested keys with underscore separators.

Store leftover pasta

How to store leftover cooked pasta?
Airtight container in fridge, 3-5 days. Toss with oil to prevent sticking.
Code review best practices
Under 400 lines changed. Anything bigger should be split into stacked PRs. Review within 24 hours. If you can't understand it from the PR description + code, the PR needs better documentation.
What is compound interest?
Interest calculated on both the initial principal and previously accumulated interest. Formula: A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt).

Home network security

How to secure home network?
Change default password, use WPA3, update firmware, set up guest network, disable WPS, enable firewall.

Explain version control
A snapshot of your changes with a message describing what you did. Think of it as a save point in a game — you can always go back to it. Write clear commit messages: 'Add user login' not 'fix stuff'.
What is a load balancer in one sentence
A traffic controller that distributes incoming requests across multiple servers so no single server gets overwhelmed.

Deobfuscate full function

def _0x3f(a):
    _0x1 = []
    _0x2 = 0
    while _0x2 < len(a):
        _0x3 = a[_0x2]
        _0x4 = 1
        while _0x2 + _0x4 < len(a) and a[_0x2 + _0x4] == _0x3:
            _0x4 += 1
        _0x1.append((_0x3, _0x4))
        _0x2 += _0x4
    return _0x1

def run_length_encode(data):
    encoded = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(data):
        char = data[i]
        count = 1
        while i + count < len(data) and data[i + count] == char:
            count += 1
        encoded.append((char, count))
        i += count
    return encoded
Explain cookies (web) in 15 words
Small text files websites store in your browser to remember preferences, sessions, and tracking data.

Cook rice perfectly

How to cook rice?
Rinse until water runs clear. 1:1.5 rice to water ratio. Bring to boil, simmer covered 18 min. Rest 5 min. Don't peek.
What is the butterfly effect?
The idea that small changes in initial conditions can lead to large differences in outcomes, from chaos theory. Named by Edward Lorenz.
What does GDP measure?
Gross Domestic Product: the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country in a specific time period.
